title: Ranganthittu Bird Sanctuary
tagline: Ranganthittu Bird Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Karnataka

overview.short: Ranganathittu Bird Sanctuary (also known as Pakshi Kashi of Karnataka), is a bird sanctuary in the Mandya District of Karnataka, in India.
overview.description: Ranganathittu Bird Sanctuary (also known as Pakshi Kashi of Karnataka), is a bird sanctuary in the Mandya District of Karnataka, in India. It is the largest bird sanctuary in the state, 40 acres (16 ha) in area, and comprises six islets on the banks of the Kaveri river. The sanctuary has been designated on 15 February 2022 as a protected Ramsar site since 2022. Ranganathittu is located 3 km (1.9 mi) from the historic town of Srirangapattana, and 16 km (9.9 mi) north of Mysore.

topPlaces.name: Palahalli
topPlaces.description: Palahalli is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Shrirangapattana taluk of Mandya district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Naganahalli
topPlaces.description: Naganahalli is a small village in Mysuru district of Karnataka state, India.

topPlaces.name: Payana Museum
topPlaces.description: Payana Museum is an automotive museum in Bangalore-Mysore Expressway, Mandya district, Karnataka, India.

topPlaces.name: Obelisk Monument
topPlaces.description: Obelisk Monument, also known as the Siege Monument, is a commemorative edifice built in Srirangapatna in the Indian state of Karnataka. It marks the memory of the British officers and native soldiers who lost their lives during the siege of Srirangapatna on 4 May 1799 during the last Fourth Anglo-Mysore war fought during 1798-99 between the British army and the forces of the Mysore Kingdom led by Tipu Sultan.

topPlaces.name: Garrison Cemetery, Seringapatam
topPlaces.description: Garrison Cemetery is located in Seringapatam, on the banks of the river Cauvery, about 300m from the Bangalore Mysore Highway. It has about 307 graves of the European officers killed in the final assault on Tippu Sultan in 1799, and their family members.

topPlaces.name: Tipu Sultan Fort
topPlaces.description: Tipu Sultan's Fort is a historical fort located in Srirangapatna, the historical capital city of the Kingdom of Mysore in present-day South Indian state of Karnataka. Built by the Timmanna Nayaka in 1454, the fort was modified by King Haider Ali & King Tipu Sultan and fully fortified in the late 18th century with the help of French architects.

topPlaces.name: Scott's Bungalow
topPlaces.description: The Scott's Bungalow is located in Seringapatam on the banks of the river Cauvery, at about half a mile from the Mysore Gate of the Seringapatam gate. The bungalow was the residence of Col.

topPlaces.name: Ranganathaswamy Temple, Srirangapatna
topPlaces.description: The Ranganathaswamy temple or Sri Ranganathaswamy temple in Srirangapatna, in the Mandya district of Karnataka state, India, is dedicated to the Hindu god Ranganatha (a manifestation of Vishnu). The temple is classified one among the 108 Abhimana Kshethram of Vaishnavate tradition.

Obelisk Monument, also known as the Siege Monument, is a commemorative edifice built in Srirangapatna in the Indian state of Karnataka. It marks the memory of the British officers and native soldiers who lost their lives during the siege of Srirangapatna on 4 May 1799 during the last Fourth Anglo-Mysore war fought during 1798-99 between the British army and the forces of the Mysore Kingdom led by Tipu Sultan.

Garrison Cemetery is located in Seringapatam, on the banks of the river Cauvery, about 300m from the Bangalore Mysore Highway. It has about 307 graves of the European officers killed in the final assault on Tippu Sultan in 1799, and their family members.

Tipu Sultan's Fort is a historical fort located in Srirangapatna, the historical capital city of the Kingdom of Mysore in present-day South Indian state of Karnataka. Built by the Timmanna Nayaka in 1454, the fort was modified by King Haider Ali & King Tipu Sultan and fully fortified in the late 18th century with the help of French architects.
The Scott's Bungalow is located in Seringapatam on the banks of the river Cauvery, at about half a mile from the Mysore Gate of the Seringapatam gate. The bungalow was the residence of Col.

The Ranganathaswamy temple or Sri Ranganathaswamy temple in Srirangapatna, in the Mandya district of Karnataka state, India, is dedicated to the Hindu god Ranganatha (a manifestation of Vishnu). The temple is classified one among the 108 Abhimana Kshethram of Vaishnavate tradition.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Ranganthittu Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Ranganthittu Bird Sanctuary is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Ranganthittu Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mysuru Airport (19 km) and the nearest railway station is Mysuru Junction (10 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Ranganthittu Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Ranganthittu Bird Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Ranganthittu Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Ranganthittu Bird Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Ranganthittu Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Palahalli, Naganahalli, Payana Museum, Obelisk Monument, Garrison Cemetery, Seringapatam.
